; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神}}, among others |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>{{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅}} * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Aikawa Station|ja|相川駅}}

== External links == {{Commonscat|Takahama-jinja (Suita)}} *[http://takahamajinjya.kir.jp/ Official website]

The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.

__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神}}, among others |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>{{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅}} * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Aikawa Station|ja|相川駅}}

The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.

__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神}}, among others |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>{{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ}}, {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神}}, among others |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>{{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅}} * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Aikawa Station|ja|相川駅}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Takahama-jinja (Suita)}} *[http://takahamajinjya.kir.jp/ Official website]

; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.

; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|12=draft}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|12=draft}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|12=draft}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|12=draft}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|12=draft}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=draft}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|12=draft}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|12=draft}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin|12=draft}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|12=draft}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|12=draft}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|12=draft}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)

; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|12=draft|12=draft}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|12=draft|12=draft}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.

In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft|12=draft}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|12=draft|12=draft}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|12=draft|12=draft}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, among others |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>{{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former Rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|qq=draft|qq=draft}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Aikawa Station|ja|相川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Takahama-jinja (Suita)}} *[http://takahamajinjya.kir.jp/ Official website]
{{translated page|ja|高浜神社|version=39622261|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[ja:高浜神社]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E6%B5%9C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%90%B9%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takahama Shrine]] [[sn:akahama Shrine]]

[[ja:高浜神社]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E6%B5%9C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%90%B9%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takahama Shrine]] [[sn:akahama Shrine]]
<!--Takahama Shrine--><!--Takahama Shrine-->

__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, among others |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>{{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former Rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|qq=draft|qq=draft}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Aikawa Station|ja|相川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

== External links == {{Commonscat|Takahama-jinja (Suita)}} *[http://takahamajinjya.kir.jp/ Official website]

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|qq=}}, among others |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>{{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Province|qq=}}, Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former Rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}} * Suita ancestral deities ({{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugita Muraji|qq=}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities {{ill|Amenohoakari|ja|天火明命|lt=Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto|Amenohoakari=Amenohoakari|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}} ({{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima District (Osaka)|qq=}}. After the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}} in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors {{ill|Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sujin|qq=}} and {{ill|Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikō|qq=}}. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōki|qq=}} founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkō Enman-ji|qq=}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suita Station (JR West)|qq=}} * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s {{ill|Aikawa Station|ja|相川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa Station|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Takahama-jinja (Suita)}} *[http://takahamajinjya.kir.jp/ Official website]
{{translated page|ja|高浜神社|version=39622261|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[ja:高浜神社]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E6%B5%9C%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%90%B9%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takahama Shrine]] [[sn:akahama Shrine]]

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|qq=}}, among others |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>[[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former Rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}} * Suita ancestral deities ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]], {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugita Muraji|qq=}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities [[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]] ({{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima District (Osaka)|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]] and [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkō Enman-ji|qq=}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Suita Station|Suita Station (JR West)]] * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Aikawa Station (Osaka)|Aikawa Station]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Takahama-jinja (Suita)}} *[http://takahamajinjya.kir.jp/ Official website]
{{translated page|ja|高浜神社|version=39622261|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}} * Suita ancestral deities ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]], {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugita Muraji|qq=}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities [[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]] ({{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima District (Osaka)|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]] and [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkō Enman-ji|qq=}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Suita Station|Suita Station (JR West)]] * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Aikawa Station (Osaka)|Aikawa Station]]

; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山権現}} * Suita ancestral deities ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]], {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugita Muraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities [[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]] ({{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima District (Osaka)|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]] and [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkō Enman-ji|qq=}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Suita Station|Suita Station (JR West)]] * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Aikawa Station (Osaka)|Aikawa Station]]

__NOTOC__ {{神社 |名称 = 高浜神社 |画像 = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|拝殿]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[吹田市]]高浜町5-34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚大神]]
[[春日大神]]
[[住吉大神]]他 |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = 5世紀前後の頃 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |例祭 = 8月5日（夏祭）
10月5日（秋祭） |別名 = 浜之宮牛頭天王社 |神事 = }} '''高浜神社'''（たかはまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[吹田市]]高浜町[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]（しましも こおり）{{small|（もとの[[三島郡 (大阪府)|三島下郡]]、太田郡）}}[[吹田村]]高濱にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[スサノオ|素盞嗚大神]] * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[春日神|春日大神]] * [[住吉三神|住吉大神]] * [[白山権現|白山大神]] * 吹田祖神二座（[[天火明命|火明命]]、[[天香山命]]） == 歴史 == [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|境内と拝殿]] [[大和朝廷]]の時代5世紀前後のころ、[[河内国]][[次田邑]]の豪族「次田連（スキタムラジ）・【新選姓氏録】には吹田連」一族が[[淀川]]を渡り、次田村を興した。これが「吹田」の始まりである。当社はその次田連一族の氏神神社で、祖神の[[天火明命|火明命]]（[[ニギハヤヒ|天照国照彦天火明串玉饒速日尊]]）とその息子の[[天香山命|天香具山命]]（高倉下命）の二柱を祀ったのが起こりである。社名の高浜とは吹田の別名である。 古代の行政区画で吹田の殆どは[[三島郡 (大阪府)|三島郡]]に属する。[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）の[[大宝律令]]で三島郡が[[島上郡]]と[[島下郡]]に分割されて以降は、島下郡に属した。吹田の北隣で同じ島下群に属する福井村には[[新屋坐天照御魂神社]]が鎮座する。高浜神社より古く4世紀後半に[[崇神天皇]]、[[景行天皇]]の勅命により奉斎、創建された。主神は'''天照国照彦天火明串玉饒速日尊'''、この神は高浜神社創建当時の主神と同じであることから'''饒速日尊'''は古代における吹田地域を含む三島郡の[[地主神]]であったようである。 『[[摂津名所図会]]』に「吹田村に二座あり。[[牛頭天皇]] [[春日明神]]を祭る。此所産土神とす。又、名就社ともいう」とある。 [[天平]]7年（[[735年]]）に[[行基]]が当社の西に石浦神宮寺を創立し、[[浜之堂]]（現、[[常光円満寺]]）と名づけると、当社はその[[鎮守神#鎮守社|鎮守社]]となり[[牛頭天王]]（[[スサノオ|素盞嗚大神]]）を勧請して、社名を'''浜之宮牛頭天王社'''とする。 また、[[清和天皇]]の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]11年（[[869年]]）、[[播磨国]][[広峯神社|広峰山]]の素盞嗚大神を[[山城国]]八坂郷の地（後の[[八坂神社|感神院]]）へ遷座させようと淀川を上ってくる途中で川が荒れ始めた。そのために近くの小島に神輿を駐めたところ、付近が疫病の難を免れたことにより里民が社殿を造営して素盞嗚大神を勧請して祀ったが、後に高浜神社に合祀された。 [[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]）から[[建長]]2年（[[1250年]]）にかけて付近には[[藤原氏]]の別邸がいくつか建築されたが、中でも高浜神社のすぐ前に[[太政大臣]][[西園寺公経]]、[[西園寺実氏]]父子の吹田山荘「[[吹田殿]]」が建てられると、[[仁治]]元年（[[1240年]]）には神境春日神社も建立されて付近は隆盛を誇った。 建長3年（[[1251年]]）には[[後嵯峨天皇|後嵯峨上皇]]が吹田殿に行幸し、その際に当社にも詣でて「来て見れば千代もへぬべし高浜の松に群れいる鶴の毛衣」と詠んでいる。 [[康暦]]2年（[[1380年]]）、当社の西に[[護国寺 (吹田市)|牛頭山護国寺]]が建立され、当社はその鎮守社ともなる。 [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]] - [[1593年]]）に戦火に巻き込まれて社殿が焼失するが、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]3年（[[1617年]]）、吹田の領主である[[旗本]][[竹中重定]]と[[郷士]]の橋本氏によって復興される。 [[延宝]]6年（[[1678年]]）には常光円満寺と護国寺が高浜神社は一体どっちの鎮守社なのかを巡って訴訟を起こしたが、[[元禄]]2年（[[1689年]]）にどっちの鎮守社でもなく吹田村の氏神であるとの[[寺社奉行]]の判決が出ている。 翌、元禄3年（[[1690年]]）、ろくんどやけ（六地蔵焼け）によって社殿が焼失するが、元禄6年（[[1693年]]）に現在の社殿が再建されている。 [[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）、[[村社]]に列す。[[1907年]]（明治40年）には[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定される。[[1908年]]（明治41年）、神境の村社である神境春日神社、白山神社、大明神社・南町の菖蒲神社を合祀する。 == 境内 == [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (2).jpg|thumb|鳥居]] * 本殿 - [[元禄]]6年（[[1693年]]）再建。 * 幣殿 - 元禄6年（1693年）再建。 * 拝殿 - 元禄6年（1693年）再建。 * 福神社（高浜えびす神社） - 祭神：[[事代主]]大神、[[大国主]]大神 * 奥津比古神社 - 祭神：[[年神|奥津彦大神]] * 奥津比賣神社 - 祭神：[[年神|奥津姫大神]] * 天満天神 - 祭神：[[菅原道真]]公 * 豊受大神 - 祭神：[[トヨウケビメ|豊受皇大神]] * 布留魂大神 - 祭神：[[石上大神]] * 笹内大明神社 - 祭神：[[稲荷大神]] * 御神木鶴の松 - 祭神：和加久寿大明神 * 社務所 明治初期まで[[神崎川 (大阪府・兵庫県)|神崎川]]右岸に位置しており、白砂青松の地として知られた高浜の森の中心にあった{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.byq.or.jp/shuppan/ryuikibook/pdf/23_Aigawa.Kanzakigawa.pdf|title=安威川・神崎川|accessdate=2022-10-07|work=|publisher=琵琶湖・淀川水質保全機構、日本水環境学会関西支部川部会、近畿建設協会}}。神崎川は水害対策のため付け替え工事が行われ新河道が開削されたが、高浜神社境内にある「鶴の松」は高浜の森があった頃の名残の株とされている。 == 交通アクセス == * [[JR京都線]][[吹田駅 (JR西日本)|吹田駅]]から徒歩8分 * [[阪急京都線]][[相川駅]]から徒歩8分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『大阪府全志』{{全国書誌番号|73012615}} *『大阪府神社名鑑』 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takahama-jinja (Suita)}} *[http://takahamajinjya.kir.jp/ 高浜神社] {{デフォルトソート:たかはましんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:吹田市の建築物]] [[Category:吹田市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[高浜神社 (吹田市)]] to [[Takahama Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

10月5日（秋祭） |別名 = 浜之宮牛頭天王社 |神事 = }} '''高浜神社'''（たかはまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[吹田市]]高浜町[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]（しましも こおり）{{small|（もとの[[三島郡 (大阪府)|三島下郡]]、太田郡）}}[[吹田村]]高濱にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[スサノオ|素盞嗚大神]] * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[春日神|春日大神]] * [[住吉三神|住吉大神]] * [[白山権現|白山大神]] * 吹田祖神二座（[[天火明命|火明命]]、[[天香山命]]） == 歴史 == [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|境内と拝殿]] [[大和朝廷]]の時代5世紀前後のころ、[[河内国]][[次田邑]]の豪族「次田連（スキタムラジ）・【新選姓氏録】には吹田連」一族が[[淀川]]を渡り、次田村を興した。これが「吹田」の始まりである。当社はその次田連一族の氏神神社で、祖神の[[天火明命|火明命]]（[[ニギハヤヒ|天照国照彦天火明串玉饒速日尊]]）とその息子の[[天香山命|天香具山命]]（高倉下命）の二柱を祀ったのが起こりである。社名の高浜とは吹田の別名である。 古代の行政区画で吹田の殆どは[[三島郡 (大阪府)|三島郡]]に属する。[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）の[[大宝律令]]で三島郡が[[島上郡]]と[[島下郡]]に分割されて以降は、島下郡に属した。吹田の北隣で同じ島下群に属する福井村には[[新屋坐天照御魂神社]]が鎮座する。高浜神社より古く4世紀後半に[[崇神天皇]]、[[景行天皇]]の勅命により奉斎、創建された。主神は'''天照国照彦天火明串玉饒速日尊'''、この神は高浜神社創建当時の主神と同じであることから'''饒速日尊'''は古代における吹田地域を含む三島郡の[[地主神]]であったようである。 『[[摂津名所図会]]』に「吹田村に二座あり。[[牛頭天皇]] [[春日明神]]を祭る。此所産土神とす。又、名就社ともいう」とある。 [[天平]]7年（[[735年]]）に[[行基]]が当社の西に石浦神宮寺を創立し、[[浜之堂]]（現、[[常光円満寺]]）と名づけると、当社はその[[鎮守神#鎮守社|鎮守社]]となり[[牛頭天王]]（[[スサノオ|素盞嗚大神]]）を勧請して、社名を'''浜之宮牛頭天王社'''とする。 また、[[清和天皇]]の[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]11年（[[869年]]）、[[播磨国]][[広峯神社|広峰山]]の素盞嗚大神を[[山城国]]八坂郷の地（後の[[八坂神社|感神院]]）へ遷座させようと淀川を上ってくる途中で川が荒れ始めた。そのために近くの小島に神輿を駐めたところ、付近が疫病の難を免れたことにより里民が社殿を造営して素盞嗚大神を勧請して祀ったが、後に高浜神社に合祀された。 [[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]）から[[建長]]2年（[[1250年]]）にかけて付近には[[藤原氏]]の別邸がいくつか建築されたが、中でも高浜神社のすぐ前に[[太政大臣]][[西園寺公経]]、[[西園寺実氏]]父子の吹田山荘「[[吹田殿]]」が建てられると、[[仁治]]元年（[[1240年]]）には神境春日神社も建立されて付近は隆盛を誇った。 建長3年（[[1251年]]）には[[後嵯峨天皇|後嵯峨上皇]]が吹田殿に行幸し、その際に当社にも詣でて「来て見れば千代もへぬべし高浜の松に群れいる鶴の毛衣」と詠んでいる。 [[康暦]]2年（[[1380年]]）、当社の西に[[護国寺 (吹田市)|牛頭山護国寺]]が建立され、当社はその鎮守社ともなる。 [[天正]]年間（[[1573年]] - [[1593年]]）に戦火に巻き込まれて社殿が焼失するが、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]3年（[[1617年]]）、吹田の領主である[[旗本]][[竹中重定]]と[[郷士]]の橋本氏によって復興される。 [[延宝]]6年（[[1678年]]）には常光円満寺と護国寺が高浜神社は一体どっちの鎮守社なのかを巡って訴訟を起こしたが、[[元禄]]2年（[[1689年]]）にどっちの鎮守社でもなく吹田村の氏神であるとの[[寺社奉行]]の判決が出ている。 翌、元禄3年（[[1690年]]）、ろくんどやけ（六地蔵焼け）によって社殿が焼失するが、元禄6年（[[1693年]]）に現在の社殿が再建されている。 [[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）、[[村社]]に列す。[[1907年]]（明治40年）には[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定される。[[1908年]]（明治41年）、神境の村社である神境春日神社、白山神社、大明神社・南町の菖蒲神社を合祀する。 == 境内 == [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (2).jpg|thumb|鳥居]] * 本殿 - [[元禄]]6年（[[1693年]]）再建。 * 幣殿 - 元禄6年（1693年）再建。 * 拝殿 - 元禄6年（1693年）再建。 * 福神社（高浜えびす神社） - 祭神：[[事代主]]大神、[[大国主]]大神 * 奥津比古神社 - 祭神：[[年神|奥津彦大神]] * 奥津比賣神社 - 祭神：[[年神|奥津姫大神]] * 天満天神 - 祭神：[[菅原道真]]公 * 豊受大神 - 祭神：[[トヨウケビメ|豊受皇大神]] * 布留魂大神 - 祭神：[[石上大神]] * 笹内大明神社 - 祭神：[[稲荷大神]] * 御神木鶴の松 - 祭神：和加久寿大明神 * 社務所 明治初期まで[[神崎川 (大阪府・兵庫県)|神崎川]]右岸に位置しており、白砂青松の地として知られた高浜の森の中心にあった{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.byq.or.jp/shuppan/ryuikibook/pdf/23_Aigawa.Kanzakigawa.pdf|title=安威川・神崎川|accessdate=2022-10-07|work=|publisher=琵琶湖・淀川水質保全機構、日本水環境学会関西支部川部会、近畿建設協会}}。神崎川は水害対策のため付け替え工事が行われ新河道が開削されたが、高浜神社境内にある「鶴の松」は高浜の森があった頃の名残の株とされている。 == 交通アクセス == * [[JR京都線]][[吹田駅 (JR西日本)|吹田駅]]から徒歩8分 * [[阪急京都線]][[相川駅]]から徒歩8分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『大阪府全志』{{全国書誌番号|73012615}} *『大阪府神社名鑑』 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takahama-jinja (Suita)}} *[http://takahamajinjya.kir.jp/ 高浜神社] {{デフォルトソート:たかはましんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:春日神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:吹田市の建築物]] [[Category:吹田市の歴史]]

__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, among others |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>[[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former Rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山権現}} * Suita ancestral deities ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]], {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugita Muraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities [[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]] ({{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima District (Osaka)|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]] and [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkō Enman-ji|qq=}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Suita Station|Suita Station (JR West)]] * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Aikawa Station (Osaka)|Aikawa Station]]

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |name = Takahama Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (1).jpg|280px|Haiden]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Suita]] City, Takahama Town 5-34 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|45|32.4|N|135|31|36.8|E|region:JP}} |Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-no-Okami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, among others |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Founding = Around the 5th century |Main hall = Three-bay Nagare-zukuri |Festivals = August 5 (Summer Festival), October 5 (Autumn Festival) |Other names = Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha |Rituals = }} '''Takahama Shrine''' (高浜神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suita]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Takahama Town<ref>[[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], Shimashimo District (島下郡), [[Suita Village]] Takahama</ref>. The shrine's former Rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.
== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山権現}} * Suita ancestral deities ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]], {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugita Muraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities [[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]] ({{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima District (Osaka)|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]] and [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkō Enman-ji|qq=}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.

== Deities ==
; Principal deities * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi-no-Kami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白山権現}} * Suita ancestral deities ([[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]], {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}})
== History ==
[[File:Suita Takahama-jinja 2021-08 ac (3).jpg|thumb|Grounds and haiden]]
During the 5th century, in the era of the [[Yamato Court]], the local clan {{ill|Tsugita Muraji|ja|次田連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugita Muraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} crossed the [[Yodo River]] and established the Tsugita Village, marking the beginning of Suita. The shrine originally enshrined the ancestral deities [[:en:Amenohoakari|Amaterasu-Kokutateru-Hiko-Honihimenomikoto]] ({{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニギハヤヒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香山命|Ame-no-Kaguyama=Ame-no-Kaguyama|lt=Ama-Kaguyama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}}, serving as the clan shrine of the Tsugita Muraji lineage. The name "Takahama" is an alternate name for Suita.
Under the administrative divisions of ancient Japan, most of Suita belonged to {{ill|Mishima District (Osaka)|ja|三島郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima District (Osaka)|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 CE divided the Mishima District into Shimakami and Shimashimo Districts, Suita was assigned to Shimashimo District. To the north of Suita, the village of Fukui, also in Shimashimo District, is home to the {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}}, founded in the late 4th century by imperial decree of Emperors [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Sujin]] and [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. This shrine shares the same principal deity as Takahama Shrine, suggesting that {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} was likely the tutelary deity of the Mishima District, including Suita.
The {{ill|Settsu Meisho Zue|ja|摂津名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Meisho Zue|qq=}} states: "There are two shrines in Suita Village. They enshrine {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牛頭天王}} and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日明神|lt=Kasuga Myōjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神}}. These are the local tutelary deities, also called Myōjisha."
In 735 CE (7th year of [[Tenpyō]]), the monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] founded the Ishiura Jingūji temple to the west of the shrine, naming it Hamanodō (present-day {{ill|Jōkō Enman-ji|ja|常光円満寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkō Enman-ji|qq=}}), making the shrine its guardian deity. [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] ({{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was enshrined at this time, and the shrine became known as Hamanomiya Gozu Tennōsha.
... (Content continues in this format, adhering to principles specified)
== Transportation == * An 8-minute walk from [[JR Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Suita Station|Suita Station (JR West)]] * An 8-minute walk from [[Hankyu Kyoto Line]]'s [[:en:Aikawa Station (Osaka)|Aikawa Station]]
